main_product.title: Sony WH-CH720N-Noise Canceling Wireless Bluetooth Headphones- White

details.title: Sony WH-CH720N-Noise Canceling Wireless Bluetooth Headphones- White
details.description:
• Sony’s lightest Wireless Noise-canceling headband ever.
  • Take noise canceling to the next level with Sony’s Integrated Processor V1, so you can fully immerse yourself in the music. 
  •  Super comfortable and lightweight design.
  • Adjustable Ambient Sound mode and Adaptive Sound control features tailors sound to suit the environment around you
  • Up to 35-hour battery life with quick charging (3 min charge for up to 1 hour of playback).
  • High sound quality and well-balanced sound tuning.
  • Crystal clear hands-free calling and voice assistant with Precise Voice Pickup technology.
  •  Multipoint connection allows you to quickly switch between two devices at once.

all_reviews.reviewText:
After wearing these all day, they're still comfortable. The fit around my ears avoids putting pressure on my ear cartilage, causing pain like on-ear styles. They are built with adult-size heads in mind and don't feel like I'm wearing a vise grip on my head. 
The equalizer is a nice amenity, but I hate the app interface %26 options. The ANC detects whether you're moving or staying still and gives an auditory indicator when it switches. If you're cleaning/cooking/or doing anything where you move for 5 seconds and stop for 5 seconds then you get the auditory “bloop” of the headphones detecting whether you're moving or still - interrupting the flow of your listening.
all_reviews.itemName: Sony WH-CH720N Noise Canceling Wireless Bluetooth Headphones - White Like New

clientResponses.response: Hi Joe, We are truly heartened to learn that you’re finding the WH-CH720N so comfortable for all-day wear! It’s great to hear that the lightweight design and over-ear fit are providing that pressure-free experience you were looking for.Regarding the "bloop" sound you mentioned, that is part of the Adaptive Sound Control feature. Since you find the switching indicators a bit disruptive while multitasking, you can actually customize this for a seamless flow. Simply open the Sony | Sound Connect app, head to the "Status" tab, and you can either toggle Adaptive Sound Control off entirely or enter the settings to disable the "Notification Tone When Switching." This way, your ANC will stay consistent (or switch silently) without interrupting your music.Thank you for choosing Sony! Regards, Dominick

clientResponses.response: Hi Mel, Thank you for sharing your feedback regarding the noise canceling on your WH-CH720N headphones. We understand how important an immersive listening experience is, and we would like to provide some guidance on how to optimize your settings using the Sony Sound Connect app.The performance of the noise canceling can often be affected by the Ambient Sound Control settings within the app. We recommend first disabling Adaptive Sound Control, as this feature automatically adjusts your settings based on your environment and movement. Furthermore, please check that the Ambient Sound Control is not accidentally set to Ambient Sound, as this mode is specifically designed to let outside noise in and will reduce the noise canceling effect. Manually selecting Noise Canceling mode in the app should provide the quiet environment you are looking for.We hope these steps help improve your experience with the headphones. If you find that you need even more powerful isolation, we invite you to look into our latest WH-1000XM6 model. This flagship version features our most advanced noise canceling technology to date, engineered to handle even the most challenging environments with ease.Thank you for considering Sony! Regards, Dominick

clientResponses.name: Gus
clientResponses.response:
Thank you for contacting Sony.

Thank you for sharing your experience and feedback about the WH-CH720N, bboi. We're glad to hear you enjoy the sound quality, but we're sorry to learn about the persistent Bluetooth disconnection issues you’re experiencing—even after adjusting the connection stability settings in the app.

We understand how frustrating frequent disconnects can be. Sony support guidance recommends a series of troubleshooting steps to help isolate and potentially resolve Bluetooth connection issues with the WH-CH720N:

- Ensure your headphones and the Sony | Headphones Connect app are both updated to the latest software and firmware versions. Updates can address bugs or compatibility issues that sometimes affect Bluetooth performance.
- Try resetting or initializing the headphones. This can resolve temporary glitches and restore the Bluetooth connection to default settings. Instructions for both reset and initialization are available in your product’s Help Guide.
- Test the headphones with a different device. This can help determine if the issue may be specific to your phone or its Bluetooth configuration.
- Confirm there is no wireless interference in your environment. Bluetooth performance can be impacted by electronic devices (such as Wi-Fi routers, microwaves), crowded wireless spaces, or physical obstructions between the headphones and source device.
- If possible, un-pair and re-pair the headphones with your phone from scratch. Forget the WH-CH720N from your phone’s Bluetooth menu and set up the connection again via the app.

We appreciate your patience and are committed to ensuring you get the most out of your headphones. If the disconnects continue after trying the steps above, please contact Sony Support with details on the troubleshooting you’ve completed, so we can help you further.

Thank you for your interest in Sony products!

Gus, Sony North America Customer Support

Thank you for contacting Sony.

Thank you for sharing your experience and feedback about the WH-CH720N, bboi. We're glad to hear you enjoy the sound quality, but we're sorry to learn about the persistent Bluetooth disconnection issues you’re experiencing—even after adjusting the connection stability settings in the app.

We understand how frustrating frequent disconnects can be. Sony support guidance recommends a series of troubleshooting steps to help isolate and potentially resolve Bluetooth connection issues with the WH-CH720N:

- Ensure your headphones and the Sony | Headphones Connect app are both updated to the latest software and firmware versions. Updates can address bugs or compatibility issues that sometimes affect Bluetooth performance.
- Try resetting or initializing the headphones. This can resolve temporary glitches and restore the Bluetooth connection to default settings. Instructions for both reset and initialization are available in your product’s Help Guide.
- Test the headphones with a different device. This can help determine if the issue may be specific to your phone or its Bluetooth configuration.
- Confirm there is no wireless interference in your environment. Bluetooth performance can be impacted by electronic devices (such as Wi-Fi routers, microwaves), crowded wireless spaces, or physical obstructions between the headphones and source device.
- If possible, un-pair and re-pair the headphones with your phone from scratch. Forget the WH-CH720N from your phone’s Bluetooth menu and set up the connection again via the app.

We appreciate your patience and are committed to ensuring you get the most out of your headphones. If the disconnects continue after trying the steps above, please contact Sony Support with details on the troubleshooting you’ve completed, so we can help you further.

Thank you for your interest in Sony products!

Please note that the WH-CH520 is not officially compatible with the PS4 via Bluetooth. There are a few alternative solutions you might consider:

positive_reviews.reviewText:
After wearing these all day, they're still comfortable. The fit around my ears avoids putting pressure on my ear cartilage, causing pain like on-ear styles. They are built with adult-size heads in mind and don't feel like I'm wearing a vise grip on my head. 
The equalizer is a nice amenity, but I hate the app interface %26 options. The ANC detects whether you're moving or staying still and gives an auditory indicator when it switches. If you're cleaning/cooking/or doing anything where you move for 5 seconds and stop for 5 seconds then you get the auditory “bloop” of the headphones detecting whether you're moving or still - interrupting the flow of your listening.

clientResponses.response: Hi Joe, We are truly heartened to learn that you’re finding the WH-CH720N so comfortable for all-day wear! Since you find the switching indicators a bit disruptive while multitasking, you can actually customize this for a seamless flow. Simply open the Sony | Sound Connect app, head to the "Status" tab, and you can either toggle Adaptive Sound Control off entirely or enter the settings to disable the "Notification Tone When Switching."
